subject: Accounting
title: Posting: Connecting Journal to Ledger
description: A quick win: understand Posting: Connecting Journal to Ledger.

hook.text: You wrote the journal entry. Now it needs to reach the ledger. That transfer is called posting.

buildup.text: Posting moves each debit and credit from the journal entry into the appropriate ledger account.

discovery.text: Reference numbers link each ledger entry back to its original journal entry—creating an audit trail.

twist.text: Modern software posts instantly. Manually, bookkeepers once spent hours posting entries each day.

climax.text: Posting is the bridge between recording a transaction and seeing its effect on account balances.

punchline.text: Journal captures it. Posting places it. Ledger keeps it.

quiz.question: What is posting in bookkeeping?
options: Transferring journal entries to the ledger
